[Nico] Hi, everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. The SAP UI MDC library is now available for productive usage, and this is since SAPUI5 and OpenUI5 version 1.124. The MDC controls, which is short for metadata-driven controls, by the way, help you build smart controls and even whole applications following the SAP Fiori design guidelines. The SAP Fiori Elements flexible programming model for RoadAW4 also use these controls under the hood. But they work with other protocols such as plain REST as well. Check out these samples in the UI5 documentation, as well as the corresponding tutorial on GitHub. Links are in the description, and I hope to see you soon.

entry.title: Client Handling in AMDP
entry.content:
What is AMDP?

  • ABAP Managed Database Procedures (AMDP) are the preferred way for developing SAP HANA DB procedures on the ABAP platform.
  • AMDPs allow you as an ABAP developer to write database procedures directly in ABAP. Special ABAP classes (so-called AMDP classes) can contain embedded code (SQLScript) that is used to generate DB procedures in the SAP HANA DB layer.
  •  AMDP is written in a database-specific language, such as Native SQL or SQLScript, and is implemented within an AMDP method body of an AMDP class.

AMDP Documentation

How can you integrate AMDP into ABAP CDS?

  • ABAP CDS provides a framework for defining and consuming semantic data models on the central database of the application server AS ABAP. 
  • Complex calculations can be pushed down to HANA through code pushdown and data can be retrieved into CDS through AMDPs. 
  • ABAP CDS acts as a container to retrieve data from database through AMDPs.
  • A CDS view has a field that cannot be calculated with ABAP SQL; then AMDP can be used to implement the calculation logic via Native SQL and code pushdown.

What features in ABAP CDS support AMDPs?

  • Table functions
  • Scalar functions

Table functions

  • ABAP CDS table functions are implemented natively on the database and can be called in CDS.
  • Table functions accept multiple input parameters and return multiple values as a tabular data set.

                    Implementation class                                             Implementation logic 

CDS Table functions documentation  

Scalar functions

  • An SQL-based scalar function (implemented with SQLScript) is a user-defined function that accepts multiple input parameters and returns exactly one scalar value.
  • A scalar function allows developers to encapsulate complex algorithms into manageable, reusable code that can then be used in all operand positions of CDS view entities that expect scalar values.
  • AMDP based scalar functions are visible and accessible in ABAP CDS views.

How is client handling handled in AMDP?

AMDP in general does not support implicit client handling. When accessing client-dependent database tables or views in an AMDP method, the required client ID must be selected explicitly. 

CDS Scalar functions documentation 

Client handling in CDS Table functions

Client handling in CDS table functions is controlled via annotations.

The CDS annotation @ClientDependent can be used to switch client dependency on and off for a CDS table function in ABAP CDS (the default setting is on).

  • The value #CLIENT_DEPENDENT enables client dependency.
  • The value #CLIENT_INDEPENDENT disables client dependency.

CDS table functions can be either client-dependent or -independent. More details on client dependency and its consequences for the CDS entitites and AMDP implementations can be found here.

Client handling in scalar functions

  • Client handling has not yet been implemented for CDS scalar functions and therefore, only client-independent objects can be accessed in the AMDP method that implements the scalar function.

What is a client-safe repository object?

A client-safe object can access data of a single client only.

  • If an AMDP method is implemented in cloud-compliant ABAP, then it is implicitly client-safe.
  • A repository object that accesses only client-independent SQL data sources is implicitly client-safe and must access only client-safe objects.

AMDP does not support implicit client handling. Therefore, client safety must be delegated to the objects accessed in an AMDP method and declared in its USING list. 

More details on the requirements for defining client-safe AMDP methods. 

client Safety in AMDP 

Client safety in CDS view entities

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 29th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/29/sap-developer-news-august-29th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP BTP ABAP Environment Release 2408

  • Release Notes Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2408/ba-p/13801154

SAP TechEd Registration

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP TechEd Session Catalog: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog

New CodeJam: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

  • New CodeJam in the topic list: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407
  • CodeJam event in Warsaw, Poland: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core/ec-p/13809704#M572

ABAP Developer Challenge and Devtoberfest

  • ABAP Developer Challenge: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/sap-developer-challenge-abap-core-data-services/ba-p/13778288
  • Devtoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-contest/ba-p/13781593

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP BTP ABAP Environment Release 2408

2:15 SAP TechEd Registration

3:08 New CodeJam: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

4:04 ABAP Developer Challenge and Devtoberfest

Transcript

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here, and yes it is time for yet again another release of the BTP ABAP environment, release 2408. And once again, this release is jam packed with new features for ABAP developers. Just to name a few, ABAP Developer Tools now supports a read-only fallback editor for development objects that do not have a native editor, for example BSPs, and also ADT now supports displaying warning messages coming from the HANA database while debugging nested AMDPs. CDS now supports external databases via ABAP CDS and ABAP SQL using CDS external entities. We also have support for logical external schemas for outbound SQL access with external entities. There has been updates to the ABAP repository object wizard, which now includes generating ABAP CDS provider models. There is also new support for creating CDS aspects to store and reuse field definitions and calculations. and we also have support for a few new annotations as well.

There are a bunch of new features around UI services in Fiori Launchpad, like support for adding a custom logo to the Launchpad and other customizing of the browser window. A lot of new features around integration topics like direct push-based integration over RFC, new enhancements around the advanced event mesh, and creating and assigning API packages.

Now I've only touched on a few of the new features to hit release 24.08. So, if you want the full story and all of the details, be sure to check out the 24.08 release blog post today. See you next time.

[Josh] It's time for SAP TechEd. Every year, TechEd is our time to get together, share ideas, learn, and have a lot of fun. Last year's TechEd was awesome, and good news, registration for TechEd 2024 is officially open. We've got an incredible TechEd lineup. First off, two full days of keynote analysis, code demos over 80 live sessions, three channels of programming, and live Q&A. That's right, they're still letting me talk live on a microphone. Then, and I am so excited about this, join us on tour as we make our way around the world with in-person stops on three continents throughout 2024 and 2025. We can't wait to connect in person for deep dive sessions, hands-on labs, and continue learning together. And trust me, this is a TechEd you will not wanna miss. See you online in October.

[Nora] Hey there, we have a new Generative AI CodeJam on the list. The first two events are already scheduled. One of them will be on the 20th of September in Warsaw, Poland at the KMD office and the other one will be right after Virtual TechEd in Munich. During the CodeJam you will learn how to access different large range models via Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core. We will use the Python SDK of Generative AI Hub and we will also use the SAP AI Launchpad to access the models. You will also learn how to implement a retrieval augmented generation use case using the HANA Vector Engine and we will have a look at different chunking techniques, how you can chunk your documents right and then we will also have a look at a multi modal model where you will learn how to work with images and text. So, make sure to also check out all the other great code gems on the list, find an event near you or host your own event. See you there! Hello, SAP developers.

[Shilpa] I hope you are enjoying the August Developer Challenge on ABAP Core Data Services. The last task in this four-week challenge was out yesterday. We have seen huge response over 550 plus submissions throughout the challenge and thanks to each one of you for taking time and as well as involving in some good discussions on best practices. For those of you who have not yet taken part in the challenge, you have one more week left until we close it. In this four-week task, you will learn about the basics of ABAP series, annotations, associations, criticality and object page. While this challenge is coming to an end, we have another exciting event kicking off in September and that is Devtoberfest. We are back again with lots of interesting sessions which ranges from September 23rd until October 25th. Like last year, we have dedicated topics each day ranging from ABAP, CAP, SAP Build, Build Code, integration, machine learning, AI, and front-end. Most of the sessions are already scheduled in the community event page. Check it out and happy learning!

entry.title: ABAP Cloud at Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
Hey everyone!

Devtoberfest is almost here - and it's Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd in 2024! 🍻

This year, Devtoberfest, the celebration of SAP technologies, is getting closer to SAP's premiere tech conference, SAP TechEd. The free, virtual event will run from September 23 to October 25, 2024, with a break during the week of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024. All sessions will be livestreamed/premiered on the SAP Developers' channel on YouTube.  

This already seems to be a tradition: Monday is 💚 ABAP & CAP Day. 

You can find a list of all the session content in the SAP Community events area: Devtoberfest Events.

  • Monday: 🟢 ABAP & CAP
  • Tuesday: 🟣 Tooling (for SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Code)
  • Wednesday: 🔴 Integration
  • Thursday: 🔵 Machine Learning, AI, and Data
  • Friday: 🟠 Frontend

ABAP Cloud is the comprehensive development model for building clean core compliant, cloud-ready business applications, services, and extensions on both SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP S/4HANA, in the cloud and on-premise. The various ABAP sessions will cover different aspects of clean core extensibility with ABAP Cloud and GenAI - including key user, developer, and side-by-side extensibility - and more.  

Take advantage of this opportunity to interact with your peers and SAP experts - and get first-hand insights into the latest and greatest ABAP features!

Here is a compact overview of the various ABAP sessions at this year's event.

◆ 📅 [CW39] WEEK 1  

  • Clean core extensibility with ABAP Cloud in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
  • Understand the clean core extensibility options for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 
  • Building partner products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment 
  • Major updates for embedded analytics in ABAP Cloud 
  • Data integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS external entities

◆ 📅 [CW40] WEEK 2  

  • Data modeling with the latest features of ABAP Core Data Services 
  • Tools for Clean ABAP 
  • SAP Forms by Adobe 
  • Test double mechanisms with ABAP Unit - Your friend in achieving effective test automation 

◆ 📅 [CW41] ABAP Cloud sessions at SAP TechEd Virtual 2024

◆ 📅 [CW42] WEEK 3  

  • What software developers should know about key user extensibility 
  • Gain Development Experience and Efficiency with Joule's ABAP Developer Capabilities
  • Developing and transforming your ABAP custom code for clean core in SAP S/4HANA 
  • How to extend the different SAP S/4HANA editions effectively and clean core compliant
  • Source-side filtering of RAP business events in SAP BTP ABAP Environment and in Advanced Event Mesh via dynamic topics
  • Discover SAP Event Sources for Advanced Event Mesh

◆ 📅 [CW43] WEEK 4  

  • Overview of reuse services in ABAP Cloud 
  • ABAP Development Tools - News and Roadmap 
  • DevOps with SAP BTP ABAP Environment 
  • SAP Business Client & SAP GUI - The Journey Continues

Below is a detailed overview of the ABAP sessions, including abstracts, schedules, speakers, and links. 
ℹ️Click on the session titles to navigate to the respective event pages in the dedicated SAP Community Group, where you can already start communicating with the speakers, your peers, and the event organizers. Indicate your interest in the session in the "Will you be attending?" and use the "Add to Calendar" option to save the date and time to your individual calendar.
🔔 You can also use the "Notify me" feature on YouTube to be notified when livestreams and premieres start!

Enjoy the event! 🍻

WEEK 1

🟢 Clean core extensibility with ABAP Cloud in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
In this session, we will demonstrate how the 3-tier extensibility model helps you to consume Business APIs (BAPIs) with ABAP Cloud in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA.
Speakers: Manikandan Rajasekaran (SAP), Vignesh Subramanian (SAP) 
Livestream:  📅 Sept 23, 2024 | ⏰ 9:00 - 10:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 Understand the clean core extensibility options for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 
Understand the difference between the different extensibility options - i.e. key user, developer, and side-by-side extensibility - available with ABAP Cloud for Cloud ERP and see how to use them. The focus of this session is on developer extensibility. 
Speakers: Carine Tchoutouo Djomo (SAP), André Fischer (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Sept 23, 2024 | ⏰ 11:00 - 12:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 Building partner products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment  
In this session, you will learn how partners can develop, build, run, and operate ABAP Cloud products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment. Get an overview of the latest features and an outlook on the roadmap. 
Speakers: Ronny Pahlke (SAP), Frank Jentsch (SAP) 
Livestream:  📅 Sept 23, 2024 | ⏰ 13:00 - 14:00 CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 Major updates for embedded analytics in ABAP Cloud 
ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) based embedded analytics provides easy-to-use analytical tooling and allows you to drill down, aggregate, and display data from your core solution modelled with ABAP CDS. In this session, we present analytical data modeling basics in CDS as well as the new multi-dimensional analysis generator for building native, multi-dimensional SAP Fiori apps on top of the ABAP CDS-based analytical data model. 
Speakers: Andreas Riehl (SAP), Olivia Baumann (SAP), Stefan Unnebrink (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Sept 23, 2024 | ⏰ 15:00 - 16:00 CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 Data integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS external entities
Data access patterns for ABAP-managed data can be categorized into data federation and data replication, as well as privileged access and business user access. Data federation allows for live access to the source system without replicating the data, while data replication involves replicating the data to a second system for local execution. In this session, we present steps for external data access from an ABAP System via SQL services and ABAP CDS based external entities. Data replication support with ABAP SQL service is also presented. 
Speakers: Arne Harren (SAP), Dr. Harish Bokkasam (SAP) 
Livestream:  📅 Sept 23, 2024 | ⏰ 17:00 - 18:00 CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

 

WEEK 2

🟢 Data modeling with the latest features of ABAP Core Data Services  
The ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) framework combines semantic data model definition with the computational power of the SAP HANA DB. In this session, we present best practices for data modeling based on the latest CDS features such as scalar functions, CDS aspects, external entities, and brand-new tool integrations such as the CDS graphical modeler for SAP Business Application Studio.  
Speakers: Matthias Herchenroether (SAP), Katharina Altrichter (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Sept 30, 2024 | ⏰ 9:00 - 10:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 Tools for Clean ABAP 
Clean ABAP helps to write readable, testable and maintainable code. While much of this requires human expertise and team discipline, some aspects can be automated. In this presentation we will show you how ABAP cleaner and the Code Pal checks for the ABAP Test Cockpit can help you to easily integrate the Clean ABAP rules into your development process and give you an update on how ABAP cleaner has evolved since its initial release last year. 
Speakers: Björn Jüliger (SAP), Jörg-Michael Grassau (SAP) 
Livestream:  📅 Sept 30, 2024 | ⏰ 11:00 - 12:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 SAP Forms by Adobe 
Find out how to make the most of print forms and interactive forms using SAP Forms service by Adobe in the cloud and SAP Interactive Forms by Adobe on-premise. We explain the overall strategy, key functions and benefits, continuous improvements and options for the cloud. We will also give an update about the local deployment option based on XSA,  the SAP HANA Extended Application Services, Advanced Version. 
Speakers: Peter Barker (SAP), Sandra Thimme (SAP)
Livestream:  📅  Sept 30, 2024 | ⏰ 13:00 - 14:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 Test double mechanisms with ABAP Unit - Your friend in achieving effective test automation 
Do you face any of the following challenges while writing automated tests in ABAP?
How to have stable automated tests? How to deal with the different types of dependencies while testing your ABAP code - ABAP classes, database dependencies via CDS, ABAP SQL, RAP BOs, function modules, role authorizations, etc? When to use which test double mechanism while writing ABAP unit tests? 
If yes, then this session can help you a bit!
Speakers: Sunil Bandameedapalli (SAP)
Livestream:  📅  Sept 30, 2024 | ⏰ 15:00 - 16:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

 

WEEK 3

🟢 What software developers should know about key user extensibility 
Key User Extensibility is a major pillar of the SAP S/4HANA Extensibility concept. It enables business experts to do last mile extensions by their own. But it also provides a lot of value for developers.
In this session, we provide an overview on the key user tools and will answer the question why developers should care for it. We provide examples for extensions that are realized as combination of key user and developer extensibility. An outlook on how generative AI support will change the creation of extensions will round off the session.  
Speakers: Thomas Schneider (SAP) 
Livestream:  📅 Oct 14, 2024 |  ⏰ 8:00 - 9:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

 

 

🟢 Gain Development Experience and Efficiency with Joule's ABAP Developer Capabilities
Learn about Joule's ABAP developer capabilities. Dive through specific use cases, delve into the generative AI product roadmap for ABAP, and gain valuable insights on leveraging these advancements to enhance your productivity and experience in everyday development tasks.
Speakers: Dr. Jasmin Gruschke (SAP), Sebastian Baskovich (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Oct 14, 2024 | ⏰ 9:00 - 10:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 Developing and transforming your ABAP custom code for clean core in SAP S/4HANA  
In this session we introduce the new guidelines for clean core ABAP development in the well-known 3-tier extensibility model in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise and handle transformation options for existing custom code to ABAP Cloud including outlook of future generative AI support. You will also learn how ABAP Test Cockpit will support governance of your clean core developments.
Speakers: Olga Dolinskaja (SAP), Thomas Fiedler (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Oct 14, 2024 | ⏰ 11:00 - 12:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 How to extend the different SAP S/4HANA editions effectively and clean core compliant
This session will explain how to extend the SAP S/4HANA editions effectively following the clean core guidance. This includes the 3-tier extensibility model, ABAP Cloud, CAP, LC/NC tools and how GenAI will support the developer. And we will explain how all this can work for customers who have a lot of legacy custom code in their system.
Speakers: Boris Gebhardt (SAP), Tim Back (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Oct 14, 2024 | ⏰ 14:00 - 15:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🔴 Source-side filtering of RAP business events in SAP BTP ABAP Environment and in Advanced Event Mesh via dynamic topics
Facilitate source-side filtering of RAP business events in SAP BTP ABAP Environment using custom context attributes comply with Data Privacy and Protection (DPP) and for more sophisticated event dispatching. Use dynamic topics for routing and filtering of RAP business events in SAP Advanced Event Mesh.
Speakers: Tatjana Pfeifer (SAP), Jonas Braun (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Oct 16, 2024 | ⏰ 9:00 - 10:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

 🔴 Discover SAP Event Sources for Advanced Event Mesh
SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh is SAP’s market leading event mesh and distributes events in real time in your SAP technical ecosystem and way beyond. Now, what is an event mesh, as sophisticated as it may be, without events? Not much, right? Therefore, we will look into how to expose events from SAP business applications like SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC and SAP SuccessFactors solutions via Advanced Event Mesh. The session will both provide the theoretical and commercial background as well as provide detailed demos of event-enabling the different backends using different approaches.
Speakers: Karsten Strothmann (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Oct 16, 2024 | ⏰15:00 - 16:00 CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

 

WEEK 4

🟢 Overview of reuse services in ABAP Cloud  
Reuse services offered by the ABAP platform provide general capabilities that are required by multiple services and apps. In this session, we will show how the most prominent reuse services that can be used in ABAP development with ABAP Cloud and which applications are delivered by SAP for monitoring and management of these services.The presentation will cover the use of application jobs, application logs, change documents, number ranges and the email API. 
Speakers: André Fischer (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Oct 21, 2024 | ⏰ 9:00 - 10:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 ABAP Development Tools - News and Roadmap
In this session, learn the latest developments regarding ABAP Development Tools ranging from the ADT actions framework that let’s you extend your development environment with custom actions over new design options and an outlook on our future IDE strategy.
Speakers: Alexander Ritter (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Oct 21, 2024 | ⏰ 11:00 - 12:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 DevOps with SAP BTP ABAP Environment
Learn about git-based customer lifecycle management (LM) on SAP BTP ABAP Environment which aims to combine proven and reliable ABAP LM benefits with the git-based CI/CD approach familiar to many. Watch live demos of our latest features and get an outlook on future developments. 
Speakers: Julie Plummer (SAP),  Dr. Christoph Pohl (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Oct 21, 2024 | ⏰ 13:00 - 14:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

🟢 SAP Business Client & SAP GUI - The Journey Continues 
Transitioning users from SAP GUI to SAP Fiori via integrating the SAP Fiori Launchpad into SAP Business Client to launch native SAP GUI for Windows transactions and Fiori Apps in one-single-entry point from local and remote systems. 
Speakers: Sandra Thimme (SAP), Ralph Resech (SAP)
Livestream:  📅 Oct 21, 2024 | ⏰ 15:00 - 16:00 AM CEST
📄💬 Access the presentation. Leave a comment. Give feedback. Ask questions. 

Have I aroused your curiosity? Then attend the various sessions to learn more about the latest and greatest ABAP innovations and best practices.

In the meantime, you can watch the replays of last year's events, SAP TechEd Virtual in 2023 and SAP's Devtoberfest in 2023. You can also check out the ABAP Cloud sessions at the upcoming SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 on October 8-9, 2024. 

We look forward to interacting with you!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 5th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/05/sap-developer-news-september-5th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Devtoberfest

  • Devtoberfest Community Page to Join/Register: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest

Five years of CPILint:

  • Five years of CPILint:
     https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/five-years-of-cpilint/ba-p/13812638
  • Release of version 1.0.5:
     https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/cpilint-version-1-0-5-is-here/ba-p/13810431
  • Devtoberfest session - 🔴 Automate your SAP Cloud Integration governance with CPILint:
     https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/automate-your-sap-cloud-integration-governance-with-cpilint/ev-p/13802890

ML and AI learning content

  • Leveraging Image Embedding and HANA Vector Engine for Reverse Product Search: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/leveraging-image-embedding-and-hana-vector-engine-for-reverse-product/ba-p/13798320
  • How Metadata Enrichment can enhance SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine on GenAI scenarios: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-metadata-enrichment-can-enhance-sap-hana-cloud-vector-engine-on-genai/ba-p/13662543
  • “Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core” CodeJam in Munich: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core/ev-p/13854907
  • "Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA" as an SAP CodeJam topic: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-quot-as-a-new-sap/ba-p/13574098
  • Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud: a time-series forecasting example: https://community.sap.com/t5/artificial-intelligence-and-machine-learning-blogs/hands-on-tutorial-machine-learning-with-sap-hana-cloud/ba-p/13683430

SAP BTP Solution Diagrams shape library in draw.io

  • New built-in SAP shape library for BTP solution diagrams in draw.io: https://www.drawio.com/blog/sap-diagrams

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Devtoberfest Promo
2:23 Five years of CPILint
3:17 ML and AI learning content
5:35 SAP BTP Solution Diagrams shape library in draw.io

TRANSCRIPT

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 5th, 2024.

[Josh] Hey everybody, it is September-October and that time period reminds me of a certain festival. That's right, Devtoberfest. Devtoberfest, an SAP TechEd event is here. We're putting it on and it starts in two weeks. So the first event is going to happen September 23rd, so please register. It's easy to register, you just join the SAP group. I'll put a link to that community page. And if you're already a member of that community page group, you're already enrolled to participate in Devtoberfest this year. And you can earn points.

And speaking of points, we have that four level game board, you can reach Nerdvana, and you qualify to be in our grand prize drawing. Like years past, people have gone on trips to major theme parks around the world as part of Devtoberfest. So by earning points and learning, you actually get a prize, possibly. So go to that community page and click join.

The other thing I wanted to point out is the number five. It's the fifth year I've had a blue beard, that I've been your blue bearded idiot. But hey, it's also the fifth Devtoberfest. Can you believe it? We've been doing this for developers, by developers for five years, but it's not just for developers, right? Anybody who wants to learn as part of the SAP community can pick a topic they're interested in and they can just complete tutorials or attend events and watch an SAP expert present to them. So five days of the week, five different topics.

Speaking of that, if you look at the calendar in our SAP community page, You'll see Monday has green circles for ABAP and CAP. Tuesday is tooling Tuesdays with a purple circle. That's fun to say. Wednesday we have red circles for integration. Thursday, don't be mad, machine learning AI and data with a blue circle. And then we have front end Fridays with an orange circle. So really, two weeks away, four great weeks of content, but Devtoberfest is five weeks long this year because we are going to stop and do SAP TechEd October 8th and 9th live from Germany. So the developer advocates, a lot of SAP experts will be doing virtual tech ed live during that week of October 8th and 9th. And then we come right back for week three of SAP Devtoberfest on October 14th. So please register. Let's pump up those numbers. Let's do as many tutorials as possible and see how many people can qualify for Nerdvana. Can't wait to see you online. Bye.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. Look what I got over the mail. Yep, a sticker of CPILint CPILint, which is an open-source tool that can help you with your Cloud Integration governance, just turned five years old. Thanks, Morten, for all the great work. And well, to celebrate it, Morten just released a new version of the tool, version 1.0.5, which now supports OAuth 2.0 to communicate with your cloud integration tenant, a mechanism to import a rules file, in a new rule to check user roles. If you want to learn more about CPI-Lint, make sure to check out the tutorial session that we have scheduled for the 25th of September, where Morten will be sharing with us how to automate our SAP Cloud Integration governance with CPI-Lint. Hasta la vista, SAP developers.

[Witalij] You might have heard about SAP HANA Cloud's new capability called the vector engine a lot this year. You might have even participated in the developer challenge, which I organized earlier this year and where we used it as well. Now at the SAP community, you will find two new blog posts describing interesting cases. So one is how metadata enrichment can enhance SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine. And the other one is how leveraging image embeddings and SAP HANA vector engine for reverse product search can be implemented.

If you are joining the new SAP CodeJam topic that my teammate Nora announced last week on getting started with the Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core, then you would use this capability as well. We have two of these CodeJams already announced, and they are available for you to sign up if you are in Warsaw or in Munich.

Getting started with machine learning using SAP HANA was quite a popular topic for code trends during the first half of this year. But even now we have already four more events scheduled for you. So if you are in Isernhagen or in Stuttgart in Germany, or if you are in Porto or Lisbon in Portugal, then please join me this month if you're interested in some hands-on exercises where you will be using SAP Business Application Studio, Python, and Predictive Analytics Library from SAP HANA Cloud to train some classification models. The other kind of model that are extremely popular among SAP customers are time series forecasting.

And my colleagues, Andreas Foster and Yannick Schaper, published recently a blog post describing how to do that, where they're focusing on one of the most powerful time series forecasting algorithms. And they're showing how to build both single and multiple time series.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. It is easier than ever to get started with SAP BTP solution diagrams. Why is that, you might ask? Well, the icons are now available out of the box in draw.io. All you have to do is click on More Shapes at the bottom of the Shapes panel in draw.io, enable the checkbox next to the SAP Shape library in the Networking section, and click Apply. Voila. All the icons are now available there for you to start your diagrams. I'm looking forward to seeing your architecture diagrams in SAP Community. Happy diagramming.

entry.title: How to display ALV GRID with only structure
SAP Developer News, September 19th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/19/sap-developer-news-september-19th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Devtoberfest Launch

  • Devtoberfest Community Home: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Events Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Gameboard: https://devrel-tools-prod-scn-badges-srv.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/devtoberfestContest/scnId.Here
  • Devtoberfest Playlist on YouTube: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d&si=R26dQIns-1e97fU0

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/aug24

Devtoberfest Crossword Puzzle

  • https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-sap-crossword.html

Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

  •  https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/transporting-content-in-sap-build-apps-sap-cloud-transport-management/ba-p/13737478

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Devtoberfest Launch
2:09 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release
7:56 Devtoberfest Puzzle
9:06 Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 19th, 2024.

[Sheena] Hello SAP community. The much-awaited Detoberfest 2024 is here, running from September 23rd to October 25th. Devtoberfest helps to prepare for SAP TechEd and offers a mix of educational sessions, hands-on developer tools, and fun activities like Petoberfest, Bugtoberfest, and Spooktoberfest. It is a point-based contest, and the Devtoberfest animated contest game board is already live. You can earn points by attending sessions, completing developer tutorials linked to weekly topics or engaging in activities that benefit the SAP Developer Community.

The grand prize is a retreat at an SAP TechEd Roadshow stop in 2025. Exciting, right? To start participating, join the Devtoberfest group after reviewing the legal terms and conditions posted there.

We will kickstart Devtoberfest on Monday, September 23rd, which is the ABAP/CAP Day. Week 1, we are bringing to you many exciting sessions in ABAP, mainly on clean core extensibility, building partner products on SAP BTP, major updates in embedded analytics, and data integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS external entities. Week 1 also covers an exciting session on CAP, it's time to TypeScript (CAP edition).

On tooling Tuesdays, we'll focus on SAP Build Process Automation, Build Code and Build Apps.

Integration Wednesdays will have sessions on FlashPy, CPI Helper, and CPI Lint.

MAD Thursdays will focus on machine learning, AI, and data with sessions on SAP Generative AI Hub, SAP Datasphere, etc.

Frontend Fridays will have sessions on UI5 Freestyle and SAP Fiori tools.

Don't forget to participate in the fun Friday event Petoberfest by posting pictures with your pets at your workstation. So join us in this fun ride of learning and enjoy. Good morning.

[DJ] The latest release of CAP August of 2024, in fact, is out. And with it, there are improvements and fixes to the latest major release 8, which came out earlier this year, in the form of CAP Node.js 8.2.1 and CAP Java 3.2.0. Let me take this opportunity on my walk this morning to go through some of those highlights.

So first up, when the latest major release of CAP came out earlier this year, the new database services went GA, including the database service for SAP HANA. One of the benefits of this new database service for the Node.js runtime is that HANA native associations were no longer required to represent associations at the CDS level. This was great, mostly because it brought longer deploy times when we used SAP HANA Native Associations, plus there was the chance of indirect dependencies also being created. And if you're wondering about the Java runtime, the Java runtime hasn't used native SAP HANA Associations for a while. Anyway, at the time, the release notes advised us to switch off the HANA native associations using configuration. With this August 2024 release, that switch is now automatic. When you start a new project and do, for example, CDS add HANA, you get a switch in the configuration that turns these native associations off by default.

In the area of the CDS language and compiler, you can now use CDS deploy dash dash script to generate a SQL script as a starting point or manual schema migration. Note that in contrast to the existing dash dash dry option, it accepts and produces DDL statements that could lead to data loss. So are A, not supported for automatic schema migration and B, contains relevant warnings in the right places.

In the Node.js runtime, there are also some important changes. One of which is that linked definitions, the class that is used to represent internally structures of services and more, now no longer have the convenience methods such as for each map filter and so on that you would normally expect to have to process the linked definitions as arrays. Because basically linked definitions are not arrays, they're more than that. So these methods have been removed. In my opinion, this is great because it reminds us that we have to treat the data as it is. And so one thing to do is maybe convert the data to arrays using the rest parameter inside, for example, an array, literal array constructor.

Another improvement is that you can now programmatically, i.e. dynamically at runtime, control and activate the CDS debug output at runtime. This means you can turn on debug for cat modules as you go.

In the Java runtime, there's now beta level support for recursive hierarchies with Odata v4 in SAP HANA. Recursive hierarchy is an experimental term in the OData hierarchy vocabulary and is used, for example, with SAP Fiori tree tables. Treetables. You can also now define the name of the Java interfaces generated for CDS entities and structured types using a new annotation. In the multi-tenancy area, activating extensions using the SAP extensibility service can now be done asynchronously. You achieve this by sending a prefer header in the HTTP request with the value of respond async. This in my opinion is a really neat way of coordinating this using the RFC 7240 standard authored by an old acquaintance of mine.

Well I'm almost home but there's just time to tell you about improvements in the tools area. So first for all you IntelliJ users out there the SAP CDS language support plugin is now available on the JetBrains marketplace which means it's directly installable now. Great! For all you TypeScript users there's a new CLI command "CDS TSX", which in contrast to the existing CDS TS command uses a different faster engine. This feature is in beta still at the moment.

So I'm back home. There's one more thing to tell you about and that is if you want to use the SAP Cloud Logging Service instead of the SAP Application Logging Service, you can do that. And there's an option to use telemetry as well, including an option to use Dynatrace. What's not to like? Anyway, that's it for this August 2024 release. See See you next time.

[Shrini and Daniel] Hello, everybody. I'm at Tbilisi, Georgia with Daniel at the EPUB office doing SAP Build CodeJam. It's really great to be here. Yeah, it's been a lot of fun and because it's only a couple days before Devtoberfest, we wanted to tell you something about the contest. So the contest you can earn enough points to get a trip to TechEd perhaps next year and one of the ways to get points is by doing the traditional SAP crossword. So there are topics about SAP Build and JavaScript and even a reference to Analytics Cloud and it would help to know something about Sabrina Carpenter. Can anybody do the Cross Word? Yes, definitely. Of course, it helps to know a little bit about SAP. There are 33 questions. Fairly easy, a few tougher ones, but I think everybody can do it, and it might even be worth a couple thousand points. And it's already published, so even before the Devtoberfest starts, you can accumulate points. So that's pretty good. That's good to know. Yeah. Good luck to everybody.

[Rekha] Hello, everybody. We all know the importance of having development, test, and productive environments during the application development. SAP has recently introduced project versioning for SAP Build apps, web, and mobile projects. This allows you to transport the released project versions between development, testing, and productive subaccounts on SAP BTP. A transport setup of Cloud Transport Management Service, CTMS, Content Agent Service, CAS, and BTP destinations are required to start transporting SAP Build Apps projects between the three tiered dev test prod landscapes. There are four types of subaccounts or tenants involved in the scenario. A source subaccount, this is where the actual development takes place. A target, under this we have two subaccounts, testing, and production, and a central sub-account to administer the content transport between the source and target sub-accounts. In order to enable the transport, you will have to enable the Cloud Foundry, subscribe to SAP Build apps and content agent services, create an instance of content agent, and generate a service key on the source and target sub-accounts.

On the central sub-account, you need to subscribe to Cloud Transport Management service, create an instance of Cloud Transport Management, and generate a service key. The MBS role collection has to be enabled in BTP cockpit to authorize the users for performing the transport activities. SAP Burn Lobby configuration has to be done in the control tower in order to set the sub-account type to decide if it's a dev, test, or prod subaccount. Then BTP destinations has to be set from source to central subaccount, and then from central to target, that is the test and prod subaccounts. Finally, set up the transport landscape using the Cloud Transport Management user interface. For more details on this topic, do refer to this interesting blog post by SAP Build expert Mari Heary. Link is provided in the description box. Enjoy learning and thank you.

entry.title: Working with Internal Sessions in ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction 

I have been writing code extensively in ABAP Cloud for some time now. As it’s a restricted version of ABAP, I encountered many challenges when trying to accomplish tasks that were simpler in the classical version. Nevertheless, some old technical aspects aren’t being replaced that easily. I’ve learned that returning to the unchanged fundamental concepts is always a good idea.

Developing in ABAP Cloud can be pretty demanding, mainly if you are used to powerful features in the classic ABAP version that are no longer available (such as using unreleased objects like CL_SESSION_INFO, functionalities like EPP passports, or more advanced tools for monitoring bgRFCs and background jobs). 

In this new programming world, I wanted to create some functionality to trace the ABAP internal sessions, spot any short dumps, receive automatic notifications, and measure execution performance. What follows is a description of how I achieved this.

 

Background

When I first started learning ABAP (too long ago), one of my main concerns was understanding the ABAP Application Server, its components, how to use its resources, and how to run the code I write in this environment. For many developers, the picture below is well-known:

Figure 1 - Sessions on the AS ABAP (from the official SAP Help  documentation)

In the classic ABAP version there are several options available for tracking the internal sessions:

  • Class CL_SESSION_INFO - which can provide a lot of valuable information like the session ID, type of user session, memory information, etc. ;
  • Extended Passport (EPP) - which can also provide information about the internal session, but is also very useful in understanding the execution from one session to another; it can also be used for automatic analysis of short dumps;
  • Others.

ABAP Cloud works with the same internal sessions, of course. A work process always executes the code you write in an ABAP internal session started by a plain old SAP program. However, these helpful tools are unavailable as they are not part of the released APIs.

Internal sessions manage SAP LUWs. Understanding how the SAP LUW concept works and how it can be leveraged helps developers create robust functionality.

In the classic ABAP version, you could use the RFC functionality in many practical ways. One of these was calling synchronous RFCs within the same system( destination NONE'), explicitly starting a new sequential SAP LUW without ending the current SAP LUW. This would easily allow you to “catch” a short dump. With the new bgPF framework in Cloud ABAP, this is not possible anymore.

Before I delve into my small utility project, there’s one more thing worth mentioning about ABAP Cloud. One of the strongest concepts introduced in this new cloud programming world, along with the Restful ABAP Programming Model, is the SAP Controlled LUW. This is a great feature but restrictive at the same time. It saves developers from manually handling transaction consistency (remember building CUD operations with CALL FUNCTION … IN UPDATE TASK?). It is by default embedded in RAP and bgPF controlled units and local consumption of RAP business events (though I wouldn’t even count the latter since it is also handled by controlled bgPF).

But what about other scenarios, like task automation or pipeline implementation and execution, where you must perform several tasks within the same ABAP internal session, split by sequential COMMIT WORK statements? If you find yourself in a SAP controlled LUW, that becomes impossible. Fortunately, you can also start ABAP internal sessions on the cloud AS ABAP that don’t “enforce” the controlled LUW part by default, or you can enforce the controlled part on your own when needed. 

These latter types of sessions are my focus in this article.

 

In summary, I wanted to be able to track in ABAP Cloud these kinds of uncontrolled LUW sessions for the following reasons:

  • There are missing tools from the classic ABAP version;
  • It is impossible to spot runtime errors automatically;
  • It is impossible to debug background jobs, so I need more info about that session, which I cannot debug.

Note: In both the BTP ABAP Cloud Environment and S/4HANA Public Cloud, I found some tools that facilitate the analysis of executed bgPF units or system work processes. However, these tools were insufficient - particularly the ‘Monitor bgRFC Queues’ app. The name itself is somewhat confusing, as bgRFC cannot be used in ABAP Cloud, where the bgPF framework is employed instead. Thus, I would have expected the term "RFC" to be avoided.

 

The solution to Track ABAP Cloud Internal Sessions

From my experience so far, I have identified the following common “types” of sessions used in ABAP Cloud (I use quotes here as this is my classification based on empirical research, not from any official SAP documentation; the list is not exhaustive) :

  • Sessions handled by dialog work processes (DIA WP):
    • Started as an HTTP call (SAPMHTTP [system]    %_http_start [module pbo] screen 0010 ) - this refers to any Odata service call as part of RAP business objects (Fiori apps) or an external HTTP call of a published service;
    • Started as a bgPF unit call (SAPMSSY1 [system]    %_rfc_start [module pbo] - screen 3004) - this is started by an explicit bgPF unit execution, or the local handling of a RAP business event;
  • Sessions handled by background work processes (BCK WP):
    • Started as a result of a background job running (RSBTCPT6    start-of-selection [event])

 

These sessions can be controlled( by the SAP frameworks themselves—like RAP or bgPF controlled launch—or by the developers using class CL_ABAP_TX ) or uncontrolled. My focus is on tracking the uncontrolled ones.

 For every session started within custom code on an AS ABAP in the cloud, I would like to know a few aspects:

  • When a session started and ended, and from what starting point;
  • Whether a session has created other sequential sessions linked to it;
  • How much time it took for a session to complete the logic;
  • If a short dump or another runtime error prevented the session from being completed.

To achieve this, I have created a relatively simple class, with a few methods, called zcl_xx_session_tracker, which can be simply used in custom code:

  • Starting: 

 

 

METHOD start.
   IF zcl_os_session_tracker=>tracker_activated = abap_false.
     RETURN.
   ENDIF.
   TRY.
       me->session_int_uuid = 
                    cl_system_uuid=>if_system_uuid_static~create_uuid_x16( ).
     CATCH cx_uuid_error.
    " This should not interrupt the processing in any way. So in case of 
       error it just exits
       RETURN.
   ENDTRY.
   DATA(stack_list) = _get_call_stack(  ).
   IF lines( stack_list ) >= 3.
     me->stack_current_point = stack_list[ 3 ]->get_text( ).
   ENDIF.
   INSERT zos_sess_track FROM @( 
                  VALUE #( uuid = me->session_int_uuid
                           session_id = me->session_id
                           work_process_type = COND #( WHEN sy-batch = 
                                            abap_true THEN 'BCK' ELSE 'DIA' )
                           stack_start_point = me->stack_start_point
                           stack_current_point = me->stack_current_point
                           started_at = me->_now(  ) ) ). 
   IF sy-subrc = 0 AND commit = abap_true.
     COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
   ENDIF.
 ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 

  • Ending: 

 

 

 METHOD end.
   IF zcl_os_session_tracker=>tracker_activated = abap_false.
     RETURN.
   ENDIF.
   TRY.
       DATA(delete_after) = cl_abap_tstmp=>add( EXPORTING 
                               tstmp = CONV timestampl(  me->_now(  ) )
                               secs  = keep_track_log_in_minutes * 60 ).
     CATCH cx_parameter_invalid_range cx_parameter_invalid_type.
   ENDTRY.
   UPDATE zos_sess_track SET ended_at         = @( me->_now(  ) ),
                             rec_delete_after = @delete_after,
                             is_finished      = 
                         WHERE uuid  = ->session_int_uuid.
   IF sy-subrc = 0 AND commit = abap_true.
     COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
   ENDIF.
 ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 

  • Linking sessions (example of linking a subsequent bgPF session):

 

 

 METHOD link_subsequent_session.
   IF zcl_os_session_tracker=>tracker_activated = abap_false.
     RETURN.
   ENDIF.
   INSERT zos_sess_links FROM @( 
                   VALUE #( parent_session_id = parent_sess_id
                            child_session_id  = subseq_sess_id
                            bgpf_mon_string   = bgpf_monitor_string
                            job_name          = jobname
                            job_count         = jobcount ) ).
   IF sy-subrc = 0 AND commit = abap_true.
     COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
   ENDIF.
ENDMETHOD.

 

 

All the information gathered with the class above is saved in custom database tables to be used and checked later.

Since I also want to catch possible runtime errors that might occur in the middle of a session, the start() and end() methods execute a COMMIT WORK by default (remember, I am using this in the uncontrolled scenario; for the controlled one, COMMIT WORK would not be possible, evidently). 

Below is a short sample of how the tool can be used for tracking sessions started from Eclipse by running the main() method:

 

 

  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

    DATA(random) = cl_abap_random=>create( cl_abap_random=>seed( ) ).
    DATA(current_session_id) = |TEST_BGPF_MAIN_{ random->int31( ) }|.

    zcl_os_session_tracker=>session( current_session_id )->start( ).

    _start_new_bgpf_udemo_unit( parent_sess_id = current_session_id ).

    zcl_os_session_tracker=>session( current_session_id )->end( commit = 
                                                                abap_false ).
    COMMIT WORK.

    out->write( 'A new test for ABAP Session tracker was started. Check 
                  also subsequent bgPF units' ).

  ENDMETHOD

 

 

All in all, this small project allows me to have a comprehensive view of the ABAP internal sessions that I start with my custom code in ABAP Cloud, as shown in the image below:

Figure 2 - Session-tracked data gathered by the tool

The information consists of the following fields: 

  • session ID - generated to identify a session
  • Work Process type -  dialog or background
  • session starting point - the first program in the call stack starting the session
  • current place in call stack - place where I call the start() method in the call stack
  • start time - timestamp
  • end time  - timestamp
  • delete record after  - timestamp
  • session is finished successfully - boolean 
  • bgPF monitor string - string generated by the bgPF framework when creating a new bgPF unit
  • job count - job information
  • job name - job information

In addition to the start and end timestamps information, I also persist the job identifier (job name and count) for the background sessions and the bgPF monitor string generated when creating a new bgPF unit. This is very useful if I want to retrieve the status of that job/bgPF unit programmatically later.

Also, I assign these tracking records a lifetime value to avoid overcrowding the database. A background job automatically deletes these records when they expire.

The above-mentioned background job is running periodically and checks the sessions that were started more than one hour ago (for sessions processed by dialog WPs) or one day ago (for sessions processed by background WPs) and have not finished yet. This is how I identify potential failed sessions and send necessary alerts when needed.

Last but not least, this functionality can be activated and deactivated according to the developer's needs as it may not always be necessary to have it active in a productive system. You can also use this tool for tracking sessions with controlled LUWs, but you must ensure that no commit work is executed, and be aware that runtime errors cannot be identified in this case.

 

Conclusion

I developed this small utility tool to help track and log internal sessions, measure performance, and identify potential runtime errors. This can also serve as a starting point for automating the maintenance of complex custom ABAP developments by monitoring ABAP internal sessions, gathering more information about them (started as bgPF or jobs), and sending alerts to the appropriate teams when vital processes exhibit unusual behavior.

As I conclude this small presentation, I would like to find out your thoughts and opinions on the topics mentioned.

 

References:

  • https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/index.htm?file=abenmemory_organization.htm
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-concepts/controlled-sap-luw
  • https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/abenepp.htm 

This article also appeared on Linked-in: https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/working-internal-sessions-abap-cloud-octavian-mihail-savu-tkmlf/?trackingId=IfI%2BV54JTt%2BuSnpwmYUveg%3D%3D

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 26th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/26/sap-developer-news-september-26th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Extensibility Wizard

  • Blog by Karthik Thirunavukkarasan, overview of Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • Blog by Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan, on side-by-side extensions with SAP Build process Automation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/creating-side-by-side-process-extensions-from-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13867657
  • Documentation:
    • Setup on BTP: https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/use-extensions?version=Cloud
    • Setup on S/4HANA Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b2ee5784ccfc45d5acf42d4c60eb5270.html
  • Antonio Maradiaga’s videos on setting up event mesh for SAP Build Process Automation:
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Delivering events from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Build Process Automation via AEM
  • Devtoberfest Session: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/s-4hana-extensions-start-fast-with-the-new-extensibility-wizard/ev-p/13795541
  • Petoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-discussions/petoberfest-2024/m-p/13872559

Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 1: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-week-1-contest-activities/ba-p/13872705
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview

  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 2: Coming Soon
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

SAP TechEd Community Organized Watch Party

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP Community Welcome: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1723237330354001PDnG
  • SAP Community Watch Zurich: https://community.sap.com/t5/z%C3%BCrich-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-zurich/ec-p/13873047#M1
  • SAP Community Watch Hamburg: https://community.sap.com/t5/hamburg-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-hamburg/ev-p/13875609
  • SAP Community Watch Sao Paulo: https://community.sap.com/t5/s%C3%A3o-paulo-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-s%C3%A3o-paulo/ec-p/13875927#M3
  • SAP Inside Track Bengaluru: https://community.sap.com/t5/bengaluru-events/announcing-sap-inside-track-bengaluru-5th-october-2024/ev-p/13855659

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Extensibility Wizard
2:09 Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap
4:03 Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview
6:56 SAP TechEd Community Watch Party

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 26, 2024.

[Daniel and Pepper] For Petoberfest, I'm here with Pepper, and we're here to talk about the Extensibility Wizard, which was released last week and let users create extensions right from within the UI of their SAP app. For the first release, it will work in S/4HANA Cloud and will let you create custom logic, custom fields known as on stack extensions or custom or extension processes known as side-by-side extensions. [Don't go to sleep.]

One of the big benefits is that it's context aware. So for example, if I'm in the sales order application, it will offer me sales order related event triggers to go with my process. There's a little bit of setup involved. So your S/4 HANA Cloud has to know about your SAP BTP and SAP Build installations. That's fairly simple, just involves destinations, trust and communication arrangements. You also have to set up Event Mesh so that your events from S/4 HANA can arrive to process automation.

Luckily, my esteemed colleague Antonio Larediaga has already created two videos to show you very simply how to set this up. One to get your events from S/4 HANA into Event Mesh and then from Event Mesh to Process Automation. The extensibility wizard will undoubtedly be the talk of TechEd and that's why we've set up a Devtoberfest session where Sesh and Kartik can talk at length about how you set this up and how How do you use it? And you will be able to ask them questions. I will put links to all of the videos, the blogs, and the Devtoberfest session in the description.

[Say goodbye.] Bye-bye. 🐶

[Thomas] It seems like just the other day we were starting our planning for this year's Devtoberfest. And here we are, nearly at the end of the first week of the four-week event. My how's time flown. Our team has been super busy both preparing for SAP TechEd virtual, TechEd on tour, as well as delivering these great Devtoberfest sessions to you. At the end of the first week, we've had a really packed week, great attendance. Up over last year, some more people are finding their way to Devtoberfest, which makes us very happy. We've had some excellent sessions across all the topic days.

Keep in mind that Monday is ABAP and CAP day. Tuesday is our day for tooling, Tooling Tuesday, where we're looking at SAP Build, Build Apps, Build Code, Build Process Automation, and how all these tools are bringing innovations for CAP development, low-code, no-code development, and ABAP development as well. Wednesday is our Integration Day. Thursday is MAD, our Machine Learning, AI, and Data Day. And then finally, Friday is both our Front-end Friday, which looks at SAP UI5 and Fiori and other topics related to front-end tools. But Friday is also our Fun Friday, where we have special activities.

Depending upon when you're watching this news item, There may still be time to enter into Petoberfest. Uh, that is our Fun Friday activity for September 27th. Post a picture of your pet in your work environment and, and you can earn some bonus points as well as have fun checking out everybody else's pet pictures as well.

Next Friday, we're going to have another special event. It's going to be our Techtoberfest day on, uh, October the 4th. And on that day, we're going to be asking you to post pictures of your work environment. Show us your laptop set up, your desk set up, whatever cool kind of thing you have set up in your work environment.

Hey, in week two, we will repeat the same daily track structure that you've that you've experienced in this first week. In fact, all four weeks will follow the same track structure, but our Monday is particularly packed with eight individual sessions across CAP and ABAP. We've got some really great things coming in that day. And it's going to be chock full of content. On Tuesday, I'm particularly interested to see the Build Apps, what's coming soon, where they're going to show both new AI innovations and the introduction of SAP UI5 output for Build Apps. Something that I've personally been looking for, for a while. We've got some cool stuff on the integration day around the event architecture and cloud events. We have content on the mad day around generative AI and using CAP with generative AI as well. So a real generative AI kind of day for that particular Thursday. And then finally, for our front end day, we have building elegant applications with Fiori for and Build Code for Android and an iOS Building applications for iOS as well. So we really have a mobile day focused on both Android and iOS

So we've got quite a selection of content for you all throughout the week and keep in mind once we finish week two we'll go directly into TechEd Virtual brought to you live from Germany And then we'll come back with more Devtoberfest, two more weeks of Devtoberfest after TechEd Virtual. And don't forget to enter into the contest. We have activities every day of the week, either by filling out validation tutorials for what you've just saw in your lecture sessions or completing tutorials that go along with them as well. So we've got lots of fun ways for you to earn points towards the big prize that you could win, win, which is, of course, a trip to one of the TechEd in-person events next year. And keep an eye out for TechEd on Tour, where from the ASUG TechConnect event, we're going to be bringing the winners from last year's Devtoberfest. We'll have some great pictures and videos from that event when it takes place in early November. So we look forward to seeing you in all the Devtoberfest and TechEd activities that are coming your way this month.

[Josh] Hey everybody. Are you as excited as I am that it is almost time for SAP TechEd? That's right. October 8th and 9th, SAP TechEd is happening this year. We're going to be coming to you live from Heidelberg, Germany. But if you can't be with us because it's virtual and you still want to talk with others and meet and watch the entire event, you can organize your own watch party. I know that several watch parties have already been organized for the SAP TechEd keynote, which is an hour and a half. It's going to be Michael Amling, Mohammed Alam, several others are going to be putting lots of great information out in this year's keynote. And right after that, for 30 minutes, me and Nora are going to get together and we're going to be hosting a community welcome.

So lots of watch parties are being organized around the world. The SAP community has several websites that I'll put links to in this video so you can see where others are getting together and maybe you can organize your own watch party as well. You can set it up, you can organize it on the SAP community website, and just let others know that you're going to be getting together to watch the Keynote and the Community Welcome and any other parts of SAP TechEd that you want to watch with others. I'll put a link to the one in Zurich, Hamburg, Sao Paulo, and then I think there's going to be one coming from Bengaluru, there's an SAP Inside Track, an SIT event that is happening on October 5th, and if you register for that, I believe you get an invite to the watch party, which is again happening on October 8th, when SAP's TechEd first day is going to go live at 9 a.m. Central European Time. I don't know where you are, so you have to figure out your own times on yourself. That's it, and I can't wait to see you from TechEd.

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #17
entry.content:
👉 The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for SAP BTP ABAP Environment send us an email and we will get back to you.
 

Introduction

 

As SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk) and ABAP Cloud became more popular inside and outside of SAP, there is a high demand for rolling out the latest product news and updates, asking questions, and showing demos. 

💡 You can find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous rroundtables in this GitHub repository.


Meeting Information

When:                                                                      
  • October 22nd, 10:00 - 11:00 AM CET  – Zoom Meeting (please register in advance) 
 
Who:
  • All interested customers, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable
  • Steampunk team: Frank Jentsch (Product Lead for SAP BTP ABAP Environment), Iwona Hahn, Burcu Karlidag, Ronny Pahlke (Product Management)

Preliminary Agenda:
  • System landscape setup and costs
  • Pre-upgrade option for 2411 release
  • Planned features for 2411 release
  • Q&A 
Looking forward to meeting you!
 


Check out our SAP Business Technology ABAP Environment page in SAP Community for product updates and upcoming events.
